expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Case focus is explicitly covered: Tavily-first then Confluence fallback ordering
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Evidence-backed checks (1)”, the output cites Phase 3 contract language requiring “spawn Tavily-first deep-research” and allowing “optional Confluence fallback ordering,” plus a global guardrail: “must run tavily-search first and use confluence only as a recorded fallback,” and reference.md policy `deep_research_policy: tavily_first_confluence_second` with Confluence artifacts marked conditional.

expectations.text: [phase_contract][blocking] Output aligns with primary phase phase3
expectations.evidence: In result.md under “Evidence-backed checks (2)”, the output explicitly ties the ordering requirement to Phase 3 responsibilities, outputs, and Phase 3 “--post” validation, listing Phase 3 artifacts/validators (e.g., `phase3_spawn_manifest.json`, `context/deep_research_plan_<feature-id>.md`, Tavily-first artifacts, conditional Confluence artifacts, and validation of “Confluence fallback ordering (when used)”).
